extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT Wout won after chasing solo when completely isolated, holding off LAURANCE Axel in the sprint after doing all late-race bridging work himself.
tactical_patterns: TRATNIK Jan attacked solo entering finishing circuit when 15-rider chase group was too large to organize effectively.
VAN AERT Wout bridged alone to front groups when isolated, revealing he can win without team support via extraordinary diesel power.
Lotto-Soudal executed full leadout for DE LIE Arnaud chasing UCI points for WorldTour survival, showing desperation tactics.
AG2R launched chase in final quarter with COSNEFROY Benoît defending title, but ultimately just delivered him into leadout role.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Won despite complete isolation and solo bridging work, signaling exceptional condition pre-Worlds despite visible fatigue.
rider_signals.LAURANCE Axel: positive: Nearly beat Van Aert at 21 years old, coming off DE BUYST Jasper wheel, marks breakthrough sprint result.
rider_signals.KAMP Alexander: positive: Danish champion took third with late surge, showing unexpected sprint capacity in reduced bunch finish.
rider_signals.DE LIE Arnaud: neutral: Fourth despite full Lotto leadout suggests inability to convert optimal positioning against top-tier sprinters.
rider_signals.TRATNIK Jan: positive: Solo attacked when chase group became unmanageable, showing tactical intelligence even though caught inside 8km.
rider_signals.COSNEFROY Benoît: negative: Defending champion relegated to leadout duty for NAESEN Oliver, indicating form decline from previous year.
rider_signals.PICCOLO Andrea: positive: Strong breakaway ride and fifth place shows EF climbing domestique has legitimate classics finishing speed.
rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: neutral: Sixth after being well-positioned suggests sprint timing issues or insufficient top-end speed in reduced field.
